title: hieracium umbellatum subsp. elisabethae
intro: Hieracium umbellatum subsp. elisabethae, also known as the musk hawk's beard, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the hawkweed plant, native to the mountainous regions of the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Hieracium umbellatum subsp. elisabethae include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent. The herbal preparations of Hieracium umbellatum subsp. elisabethae are typically made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
